An eCommerce website is a website from which you can buy orsell physical or digital products online. This can include a digital storefront, product galleries, product pages,an online shopping cart, online checkout, and all of the backend features you need to manage payment processing, shipping, customer support and eCommerce marketing.
In order to build an eCommerce website, you need the following:
A domain name- Your domain name, which is your site’s unique URL, should reflect your brand.
Hosting- This is the infrastructure on which your website is maintained. You should seek reliableecommerce hostingthat ensures your site maximum uptime.
Products to sell- You can sell your own physical or digital products and services, or sell ready-to-ship products from a dropshipping supplier.
Payment provider- This is a company that enables you toaccept payments. Ideally, your payment provider should let you accept multiple payment methods.
Online store theme/template and design- Sign up for an eCommerce website builder that allows you to choose a suitable template and customize it.
With Wix, you can get all of these tools for building an eCommerce website in one place.

Wix provides secure and freeweb hostingas soon as you publish your site, even if it’s a free e-commerce website without a Premium plan. Combined with Wix’s own data centers, your site runs on the unified power of Google Cloud, AWS and Fastly. Your Wix site is served by four data centers across three continents, and is reinforced by over 200 global content delivery network (CDN) nodes. This multi-cloud hosting improves your load times and provides full global coverage.
AI can be used in a number of ways to increase your e-commerce website’s reach and revenue.Wix features several AI toolswhich can help you increase traffic, engagement and sales, including:

AI product description generator -This tool allows you to enter simple prompts about your products and Wix's AI Text Creator will quickly generate high-quality product descriptions, saving time and effort, and uplifting conversion and SEO.

Twik AI-powered site personalization -This smart AI engine shows store visitors a personalized product arrangement tailored to their shopping habits. On average, Twik users see a revenue uplift of 12% after using the app.

Smart product recommendations- You can increase your average order value with AI-based product recommendation sections, displaying related products personalized to your customer before checkout. Algorithms include frequently bought together, bestsellers, and more.

AI image creator- This tool allows you to create personalized image content for your online store by simply providing  a prompt and applying your preferred image style. Having high-quality images on your e-commerce website is a key to increasing sales.

The cost of creating an eCommerce websitecan vary depending on the size of your site, the features you need and whether you hire a professional to design your site or do it yourself on an all-inclusive platform.

When you take into account design costs, the costs of feature plug-ins, hosting costs and domain costs, you can pay anywhere from an estimated $1,000 to over $10,000 to build and maintain an e-Commerce website.

If you opt to build a website on a platform that includes domain, hosting and feature costs, the price will generally be much less. Depending on the platform you choose, and how advanced a plan you need, monthly prices range from about $20 to $200 per month. For example, Wix plans for eCommerce range from $27 to $159 per month.

Building an eCommerce website on Wix is free. However, once you launch your site and want to start accepting payments from shoppers, you’ll have to opt for a paid subscription. Wix offers several annual eCommerce plans ranging from $29/month for basic eCommerce to $159/month for more advanced capabilities*. All plans come with free hosting, a free new domain for one year, Wix ‘s drag-and-drop website editor, built-in features to manage your business’s online operations and marketing, and 24/7 customer care.

*All pricing and information are current as of November 2025 and reflect prices in the US. Prices do not include applicable taxes, which are determined according to your billing address. The final price can be seen on the purchase page, before payment is completed.

There are several factors to choosing a great eCommerce website builder for your business. You should consider an eCommerce platform which not only helps youcreate an online store, but also features a full set of eCommerce and AI tools that are sophisticated yet easy to use. Features to look for in an eCommerce website builder include the following:
A no-code, drag and drop website editor that allows you to build a unique eCommerce website quickly and easily without spending money on a developer.
Easy-to-use AI that helps you generate your brand look and feel, design your storefront, write on-brand copy and create product imagery. This lets you refine and launch your eCommerce store faster.
A platform that comes with free and secure hosting included, with guaranteed reliability and performance.
Built-in eCommerce tools for managing, marketing and running your business, with no need for extra plug-ins that cost money and require updates.

Multichannel sales integrations that allow you to promote and sell your products on platforms like Google, Facebook, Amazon, and other channels, while managing inventory and orders from one dashboard.
Competitive pricing with no surprise fees and extras.

Wix’s eCommerce web builder fills all of these requirements, offering a full eCommerce solution with AI-powered tools ideal for both eCommerce businesses who are just starting, and also for established businesses looking to grow even bigger.

Yes, with Wix you can create as many eCommerce websites for free as you want with an unlimited free trial. This includes building your website with Wix’s no-code ecommerce software, adding products, setting up shipping and publishing your site. Once you’re all set up and ready to start accepting payments on your site, you will have to upgrade to a premium plan. However, there’s no obligation to upgrade if you only want to build an eCommerce website and experiment with Wix’s platform. This is the advantage of opting for a platform with an unlimited free trial.
There are several types of eCommerce websites:

eCommerce websites that sell their own physical products- The most common type of store is one that sells its own physical products. Examples of physical products are clothing items, or kitchen accessories. In this type of site, customers pay for their purchases online through the website, and then can either pick up their items in person, or have them shipped to their location.

Dropshipping websites -While dropshipping websites also sell physical products, the site owner doesn’t supply the products themselves. In this case the site features products from a dropshipping supplier. When the customer makes a purchase from the site, the supplier ships the product directly to the customer. The owner doesn’t have to hold inventory or handle shipping in dropshipping eCommerce. They merely have to display the products on their site and attract customers. To learnhow to start a dropshipping business, check out our blog.

Print-on-demand websites -Print-on-demand is a form of dropshipping eCommerce in which the supplier prints the site owner’s designs or logo on products such as t-shirts, mugs, or more exotic products like headphones and chef’s knives in the case of Wix’s native print-on-demand integration. Just as with dropshipping, the supplier handles the shipping and fulfillment to the end customer.

eCommerce websites that sell digital products -Stores that sell digital products sell downloadable or viewable products that do not need to be shipped. This can include ebooks, online courses, music, gift cards, and more.

On a platform such as Wix, you don’t have to choose only one of these types of eCommerce sites. In fact, you can offer your own physical and digital products, as well as dropshipping and print-on-demand products all on one site and manage it all from your Wix dashboard.

Choosing the right name for your eCommerce website is an essential part of establishing your business. Pick a name that best fits your brand. If you’re looking for inspiration, use ourBusiness Name Generator. Type in your business’ industry, any keywords relevant to your brand, and click the generate name button to get a list of business name ideas instantly. Similarly, you can use ourDomain Name Generatorto instantly get custom domain name ideas for your eCommerce store.

Yes. Wix is a no-codefree website builder, so you don’t need to be a designer or developer tobuild an eCommerce businesson our platform. You can create your eCommerce website entirely from a blank canvas or customize any of our designer-made templates for your retail business needs. Then simply add the features you need with native and third-party apps and integrations.If you need help with any aspect of Wix eCommerce, you can contactcustomer care.If you want some professional help to build your eCommerce store, you can hire an expert designer or developer via theWix Marketplace.
Wix has built-in tools and AI features to help you promote your eCommerce business both organically and through paid ads. These tools include:

SEO tools- to help you get discovered on search engines, including an integration with Google Search Console to see how many clicks and impressions you're getting for what keywords directly from your Wix dashboard. You can also use AI to create optimized title tags, meta descriptions and engaging blog content that answers exactly what your potential customers are searching for.

AI Visibility Overview-  to see how your eCommerce site is mentioned in LLMs like ChatGPT, Gemini and Perplexity. See which questions people ask before landing on your site and update your content to match their needs better.

Email marketing features- to help promote new products, announce sales, offer coupons and more. You can also use AI to quickly create branded emails and adapt content for different customer segments so each audience gets a version that feels right for them.

Paid ads-You can also drive customers to your store with paid ads on Google, Facebook and Instagram, all created and managed directly from your Wix dashboard. In addition, you can drive business to your store with TikTok paid ads by easily adding your products on Wix to TikTok's Catalog Manager.
Here are 6 simple steps to create your eCommerce site and start to sell products online fast:
1.Choose your platform and plan

Start by picking an eCommerce website builder that includes hosting, security, payments and store management. With Wix, you get all these and it’s free to start. Upgrade whenever you’re ready to begin accepting payments.
2.Plan your eCommerce website design

Decide how you want your store to look and how shoppers will move through it. On Wix you can choose a ready-made store template or use AI to create a custom layout. Then adjust colors, fonts and sections so your design matches your brand.
3.Add your products and store content

Create clear product pages so customers know exactly what they’re buying. Add titles, descriptions, prices and high-quality images. Adjust image sizes or color, manage stock and group items into collections to make browsing and buying easier.
4.Set up payments and checkout

Connect a payment provider so you can accept credit cards and other popular payment methods. Then customize your checkout details, including taxes, shipping options and your store policies (like returns and refunds) so they follow local laws and match how you handle orders.
5.Configure shipping and key store settings

Set shipping regions and rates, offer options like local delivery or pickup if relevant and define handling times. Add your contact details, terms and return policy so shoppers know what to expect when they buy from you.
6.Preview, publish and start selling

Before you go live, test your site on desktop and mobile. Click through product pages, your cart and checkout to make sure everything works smoothly. When it looks right, publish your eCommerce store and use built-in SEO, email marketing and social media features to bring in traffic and sell products online.

Yes. You can connect PayPal, Stripe and over 70 other payment providers to your Wix eCommerce site, depending on where your business is based. You can also use Wix Payments, our built‑in solution, to accept major credit and debit cards plus digital wallets like Apple Pay and Google Pay.

When you set up your store, you choose which payment methods to offer and connect them in your Wix dashboard. You can even use more than one provider at the same time (for example Wix Payments plus PayPal), so your store can give customers several secure ways to pay at checkout.
Most modern eCommerce platforms are secure for handling customer payments, as long as they follow industry standards and you choose trusted providers.
When you compare platforms or eCommerce software, look for:
PCI DSS compliance– This is the global security standard for processing credit cards. Your provider should be PCI compliant so you don’t have to handle raw card data yourself.
HTTPS and SSL encryption– Your online store URL should start with https:// and show a lock icon so login details and payment info are encrypted in transit.
Secure payment gateways– Using trusted providers (like major credit card processors, PayPal and other well‑known gateways) adds extra layers of security and fraud checks.
Built‑in fraud protection– Tools like address and CVV checks and extra authentication (such as 3D Secure where supported) help reduce chargebacks and suspicious orders.
Ongoing security maintenance– Regular security updates and monitoring for unusual activity are key to keeping customer data safe.

With Wix, your store is protected by built‑in SSL encryption and payments processed through PCI‑compliant providers, including Wix Payments (where available). Sensitive card data is handled by certified systems and not stored on your site, so you can focus on running your business while we take care of payment security in the background.

The easiest way to calculate taxes on your eCommerce website is to automate sales tax and VAT based on each customer’s location. With Wix, you canset up automated tax calculationfrom your dashboard so the correct rate is applied at checkout, without you having to track changing tax rules or do the math yourself.
Choose the countries or states where you need to collect tax
Let Wix automatically calculate and apply the right rate to eligible orders
Set different rules for products that are taxed differently or tax‑exempt

If you prefer, you can also enter tax rates manually instead of using automation. Wix makes it much easier to handle tax, but you should still check with a tax professional or local authority to confirm where you’re required to collect tax before you start selling.

Wix is a website builder that lets any business or individual build their own professional website. By combining design tools and business solutions in one AI-powered platform, Wix makes it easy for anyone to create without limits and scale confidently online.

There are several types of eCommerce websites: eCommerce websites that sell their own physical products- The most common type of store is one that sells its own physical products. Examples of physical products are clothing items, or kitchen accessories. In this type of site, customers pay for their purchases online through the website, and then can either pick up their items in person, or have them shipped to their location. Dropshipping websites -While dropshipping websites also sell physical products, the site owner doesn’t supply the products themselves. In this case the site features products from a dropshipping supplier. When the customer makes a purchase from the site, the supplier ships the product directly to the customer. The owner doesn’t have to hold inventory or handle shipping in dropshipping eCommerce. They merely have to display the products on their site and attract customers. To learnhow to start a dropshipping business, check out our blog. Print-on-demand websites -Print-on-demand is a form of dropshipping eCommerce in which the supplier prints the site owner’s designs or logo on products such as t-shirts, mugs, or more exotic products like headphones and chef’s knives in the case of Wix’s native print-on-demand integration. Just as with dropshipping, the supplier handles the shipping and fulfillment to the end customer. eCommerce websites that sell digital products -Stores that sell digital products sell downloadable or viewable products that do not need to be shipped. This can include ebooks, online courses, music, gift cards, and more. On a platform such as Wix, you don’t have to choose only one of these types of eCommerce sites. In fact, you can offer your own physical and digital products, as well as dropshipping and print-on-demand products all on one site and manage it all from your Wix dashboard. Here are 6 simple steps to create your eCommerce site and start to sell products online fast: 1.Choose your platform and plan Start by picking an eCommerce website builder that includes hosting, security, payments and store management. With Wix, you get all these and it’s free to start. Upgrade whenever you’re ready to begin accepting payments. 2.Plan your eCommerce website design Decide how you want your store to look and how shoppers will move through it. On Wix you can choose a ready-made store template or use AI to create a custom layout. Then adjust colors, fonts and sections so your design matches your brand. 3.Add your products and store content Create clear product pages so customers know exactly what they’re buying. Add titles, descriptions, prices and high-quality images. Adjust image sizes or color, manage stock and group items into collections to make browsing and buying easier. 4.Set up payments and checkout Connect a payment provider so you can accept credit cards and other popular payment methods. Then customize your checkout details, including taxes, shipping options and your store policies (like returns and refunds) so they follow local laws and match how you handle orders. 5.Configure shipping and key store settings Set shipping regions and rates, offer options like local delivery or pickup if relevant and define handling times. Add your contact details, terms and return policy so shoppers know what to expect when they buy from you. 6.Preview, publish and start selling Before you go live, test your site on desktop and mobile. Click through product pages, your cart and checkout to make sure everything works smoothly. When it looks right, publish your eCommerce store and use built-in SEO, email marketing and social media features to bring in traffic and sell products online. Yes. You can connect PayPal, Stripe and over 70 other payment providers to your Wix eCommerce site, depending on where your business is based. You can also use Wix Payments, our built‑in solution, to accept major credit and debit cards plus digital wallets like Apple Pay and Google Pay. When you set up your store, you choose which payment methods to offer and connect them in your Wix dashboard. You can even use more than one provider at the same time (for example Wix Payments plus PayPal), so your store can give customers several secure ways to pay at checkout. Most modern eCommerce platforms are secure for handling customer payments, as long as they follow industry standards and you choose trusted providers. When you compare platforms or eCommerce software, look for: PCI DSS compliance– This is the global security standard for processing credit cards. Your provider should be PCI compliant so you don’t have to handle raw card data yourself. HTTPS and SSL encryption– Your online store URL should start with https:// and show a lock icon so login details and payment info are encrypted in transit. Secure payment gateways– Using trusted providers (like major credit card processors, PayPal and other well‑known gateways) adds extra layers of security and fraud checks. Built‑in fraud protection– Tools like address and CVV checks and extra authentication (such as 3D Secure where supported) help reduce chargebacks and suspicious orders. Ongoing security maintenance– Regular security updates and monitoring for unusual activity are key to keeping customer data safe. With Wix, your store is protected by built‑in SSL encryption and payments processed through PCI‑compliant providers, including Wix Payments (where available). Sensitive card data is handled by certified systems and not stored on your site, so you can focus on running your business while we take care of payment security in the background. The easiest way to calculate taxes on your eCommerce website is to automate sales tax and VAT based on each customer’s location. With Wix, you canset up automated tax calculationfrom your dashboard so the correct rate is applied at checkout, without you having to track changing tax rules or do the math yourself. Choose the countries or states where you need to collect tax Let Wix automatically calculate and apply the right rate to eligible orders Set different rules for products that are taxed differently or tax‑exempt If you prefer, you can also enter tax rates manually instead of using automation. Wix makes it much easier to handle tax, but you should still check with a tax professional or local authority to confirm where you’re required to collect tax before you start selling.
